Alright, your back time to roll forward. What I want to focus on now is one of the most crucial aspects of mastering the straight line system. What I'm talking about here is something called rapport building. And rapport in my mind is probably the most misunderstood and most abused word in the English language, especially in the context of sales. Because what most salespeople think Casus rapport is actually repulsive to people, meaning it repels them, doesn't attract them. See rapport is that attractive force where, you know, it's this alignment where two people see things the same way. But at the very heart of rapport, what it's really about is two things. Number one, when they feel for some just inexplicable reasons, unconscious, right below the surface, not logical, that they feel that you care. You care about them. It's not just about you trying to make a commission, sell them a product, do a service. No, you actually care about their needs, about feeling needs, resolving pain. And when someone feels that way, wow, it's like just imagine the ramifications are awesome. Because if someone senses that, you know, you really care, they're like, you know, well, maybe he's not your average salesperson. Maybe she's not just here to make a commission check. She actually cares about me. That's really the basis of rapport, that you care. And the second aspect of rapport building is that you're just like them, just like you. Now not in the sense that whatever they say they like, you like, oh, I like sword fishing. Oh my God, I like sword fishing too. They like, oh, I like battle to me too. You like to shoot guns. Me too. That's not what I mean by I'm just like you. I'm talking about that you speak in a similar rate of speech that you understand. Ah, yep, got it. Yep. And it's those, the unconscious level of communication, the ah-has, the ums, the yups, where you actually up, we understand things. I see the world the same way. In other words, here's the deal. You know, this idea that, you know, why do people attract each other? They say, well, opposites attract. No, that's bullshit. It's not true. Opposites don't attract. I mean, just look at it this way. Imagine if you came home to your husband or to your wife, let's say you're a man, come on to my wife. Honey, guess what? I just went to this country club. Okay? They all are the opposite political party on me. I love playing tennis. They all love playing golf here. We have absolutely nothing in common. So I joined. I should be like, what the hell is wrong with you? Why would you do that, right? You would never, you wouldn't do that. You know, you wouldn't just associate somewhere because of all the opposites. Rather, what I would do is I would come and say, hey, I guess what? I went, oh my God, they all love tennis. Same political views as me. Very similar interests. We got a little, and I joined. You say, oh wow, that makes some sense, right? In other words, this idea that I'm just like you. That we generally see the world the same way. That and I care about you are these two linchpins of rapport. And just remember, rapport is not built by you walking into someone's office and you see the fish on the wall and you're like, oh my God, I love fishing. Who's that trout fishing? I love trout. Where? Last, oh my God, last is my favorite. That's not when I say just like you. I don't mean you share every interest. That's just complete bullshit. It's disingenuous. People are way too smart for that. Now obviously, if there is some commonality that's just on the nose, yeah, of course, you could talk about that in the context of what you're trying to accomplish, what your outcome is. But don't go off to Pluto. This is what going off to Pluto means, where you just start talking about the price of tea and China or whatever they happen to say because you believe that if your prospect mentioned they like fly fishing, they used to say, oh my God, fly fishing is my favorite. That's not rapport. Rapport is based on this unconscious, this sense that this person cares about me. They're listening to me. They're listening. You have two ears, one mouth. You want to listen twice as much as you speak. This is so crucial here. When we get to this really when I talk about prospecting and how you actually gather intelligence remember though that at the end of the day rapport is based on the fact that you care and feel your pain also is part of that. You're going to care about your needs, right? You're not just going to make money and also just like you. We see things basically the same way. So if someone's talking really, really fast, you want to speed up your speech if they're talking slow, you slow down. These are all the finer points of tonality and unconscious communication. Just remember for most people what happens with unconscious communication is that their words come out okay. Especially if I give you a script, you learn to read the words and the words are great. They're on target, right? People can accomplish that. But what often happens is there's some floor in your own internal communication platform while the words come out fine, the appropriate tonality doesn't follow a long suit. You think you sound good, but you actually don't. Remember the purpose of this lesson, of this module in general is to begin the process of showing you how to apply these specific tonalities at those exact right moments in your presentation. So when you have to sound absolutely certain, you sound absolutely certain. When you have to sound utterly sincere, you'll sound totally sincere. And when you have to sound totally reasonable, you sound totally reasonable. Bottom line is that's what this training is all about because once you can apply these tonalities correctly, you will be shocked at how easy influencing becomes. And with that, it's now quiz time. So take your quiz, answer your questions, go through your exercises and when you're done, I'll meet you in the next lesson. I'll see you there.
